sections.paragraph:
(“the midst of the places of quiet”), pr.n. m., 1Ch 2:52 whence patron. חֲצִי הַמְּנַחְתִּי verse 54 1Ch 2:54.

I. חָצִיר i.q. חָצֵר prop. a fence; hence poetically a habitation, dwelling, i.q. בַּיִת. Isa 34:13 [a dwelling for ostriches, 35:7 .], “a dwelling for reeds and rushes.” Root חָצַר No. I.

II. חָצִיר m.-

(1) grass, Job 8:12 40:15 Psa 104:14.-(2) a leek, Num 11:5. Root חָצַר No. II.

[“חָצַן an unused root, whose primary power appears to have been that of strength and firmness; compare Arab. حَصَنَ to be strong, to be fortified.”]
